docs.title: LeTorch
docs.text:
PyTorch-native tools for neural representation analysis.

LeTorch currently provides:

  • RSA (Representation Similarity Analysis)
  • CKA (Centered Kernel Alignment)
  • CDNV (Class-Distance Normalized Variance)

docs.title: Alignment Quick Start
docs.text:
import torch
from repx import RSA, CKA

X = torch.randn(50, 512)
Y = torch.randn(50, 768)

rsa = RSA(rdm_metric="correlation", compare="spearman")
cka = CKA(kernel="linear")

print(rsa.rsa(X, Y).item())
print(cka.cka(X, Y).item())

docs.title: Geometry Quick Start
docs.text:
import torch
from repx.geometry import compute_cdnv, compute_directional_cdnv

X = torch.randn(100, 128)
y = torch.randint(0, 10, (100,))

print(compute_cdnv(X, y, num_classes=10))
print(compute_directional_cdnv(X, y, num_classes=10))

For full API docs pulled from docstrings, see Alignment, Accuracy, Core, and Geometry.

docs.title: Alignment
docs.text:
Alignment-based representation metrics.

OverviewSubmodulesFiles

Alignment-based representation metrics.

This subpackage exposes: - RSA: Representation Similarity Analysis. - CKA: Centered Kernel Alignment.

  • repx.alignment.rsa
  • repx.alignment.cka
  • src/repx/alignment/__init__.py
  • src/repx/alignment/rsa.py

docs.title: RSA
docs.text:
Representational Similarity Analysis in PyTorch.

Compares two representation spaces by correlating their Representational Dissimilarity Matrices (RDMs).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default rdm_metric ('correlation', 'cosine', 'euclidean', 'cityblock')

Pairwise distance metric used when building RDMs. Options: "correlation" (1 − Pearson r), "cosine" (1 − cosine similarity), "euclidean" (L2 distance), and "cityblock" (L1/Manhattan distance).

"correlation" compare ('spearman', 'pearson')

Correlation method used when comparing RDM vectors.

"spearman" Notes
  • Identical representations produce scores near 1.
  • Independent representations usually produce scores near 0.

docs.title: compute_rdm(X)
docs.text:
Compute the Representational Dissimilarity Matrix (RDM).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default X (Tensor, shape(n_samples, n_features))

One row per sample.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description rdm (Tensor, shape(n_samples, n_samples))

Symmetric matrix with zero diagonal.

Examples:

import torch
from repx.alignment import RSA

X = torch.randn(5, 3)
rdm = RSA(rdm_metric="correlation").compute_rdm(X)
print(rdm.shape)  # torch.Size([5, 5])

docs.title: rdm_upper_tri(rdm)
docs.text:
Extract the strict upper-triangle of an RDM as a flat 1-D vector.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default rdm (Tensor, shape(n, n)) required

Returns:

Name Type Description vec (Tensor, shape(n * (n - 1) // 2))

Examples:

import torch
from repx.alignment import RSA

rdm = torch.tensor(
    [[0.0, 1.0, 2.0], [1.0, 0.0, 3.0], [2.0, 3.0, 0.0]]
)
vec = RSA().rdm_upper_tri(rdm)
print(vec)  # tensor([1., 2., 3.])

docs.title: rsa(X, Y)
docs.text:
Compute Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA).

Builds an RDM for each representation matrix, vectorises the upper triangles, and correlates them using the settings from __init__.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default X Tensor, shape (n_samples, n_features_*)

Row-per-sample matrices. Both must have the same number of rows; feature dimensionalities may differ.

required Y Tensor, shape (n_samples, n_features_*)

Row-per-sample matrices. Both must have the same number of rows; feature dimensionalities may differ.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description r scalar Tensor

Correlation coefficient in [−1, +1]. Call .item() to get a Python float.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If X and Y have different number of samples.

Examples:

import torch
from repx.alignment import RSA

rsa = RSA(rdm_metric="correlation", compare="spearman")
X = torch.randn(20, 64)
Y = torch.randn(20, 128)

score = rsa.rsa(X, Y)
print(score.shape)  # torch.Size([])

docs.title: CKA
docs.text:
Centered Kernel Alignment in PyTorch.

Compares two representation spaces with centered kernel alignment using the debiased HSIC estimator.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default kernel str

Kernel used to build the similarity matrix.

"linear" (default) K = X @ Xᵀ. Invariant to orthogonal transformations and isotropic scaling of the rows.

'linear' Notes
  • Invariant to orthogonal transforms and isotropic scaling with linear kernel.
  • Requires at least 4 stimuli (rows).

docs.title: compute_kernel(X)
docs.text:
Compute the kernel (Gram) matrix for a set of representations.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default X (Tensor, shape(n_samples, n_features))

One row per stimulus.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description K (Tensor, shape(n_samples, n_samples))

Symmetric positive semi-definite kernel matrix.

Examples:

import torch
from repx.alignment import CKA

X = torch.randn(4, 3)
K = CKA().compute_kernel(X)
print(K.shape)  # torch.Size([4, 4])

docs.title: cka(X, Y)
docs.text:
Compute Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA).

Builds a kernel matrix for each representation, zeros the diagonals, and computes the normalised debiased HSIC.

Requires at least 4 stimuli (rows) due to the n*(n-3) denominator in the unbiased HSIC estimator.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default X Tensor, shape (n_samples, n_features_*)

Row-per-sample matrices. Both must have the same number of rows; feature dimensionalities may differ.

required Y Tensor, shape (n_samples, n_features_*)

Row-per-sample matrices. Both must have the same number of rows; feature dimensionalities may differ.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description score scalar Tensor

CKA score. Returns 1.0 for identical representations, ≈ 0.0 for independent ones. Call .item() for a Python float.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If X and Y have different number of samples or n < 4.

Examples:

import torch
from repx.alignment import CKA

cka = CKA(kernel="linear")
X = torch.randn(20, 64)
Y = torch.randn(20, 128)

score = cka.cka(X, Y)
print(score.shape)  # torch.Size([])

docs.title: compute_cdnv
docs.text:
Compute class-distance normalized variance (CDNV).

For each unordered class pair (c1, c2), this computes:

  • class variance proxy: E[||x||^2] - ||E[x]||^2 for each class,
  • average variance across the two classes,
  • normalized by squared distance between class means.

The final result is averaged over num_classes * (num_classes - 1) / 2 possible class pairs.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default features (Tensor, shape(num_samples, feature_dim))

Embedding matrix.

required labels (Tensor, shape(num_samples))

Class labels for each sample.

required num_classes int

Number of classes. If omitted, inferred as labels.max() + 1.

None device str or device

Device used for metric computation.

"cpu" eps float

Small positive value to avoid division by zero for degenerate class pairs.

1e-12

Returns:

Type Description float

Average CDNV value.

Examples:

import torch
from repx.geometry import compute_cdnv

x = torch.randn(100, 128)
y = torch.randint(0, 10, (100,))

score = compute_cdnv(x, y, num_classes=10)
print(score)

docs.title: compute_directional_cdnv
docs.text:
Compute directional CDNV.

Directional CDNV measures, for ordered class pairs (c1, c2), the variance of class-c1 features projected onto the direction from mean(c1) to mean(c2), normalized by squared inter-class distance.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default features (Tensor, shape(num_samples, feature_dim))

Embedding matrix.

required labels (Tensor, shape(num_samples))

Class labels for each sample.

required means (Tensor, shape(num_classes, feature_dim))

Precomputed class means. If omitted, class means are computed from features and labels.

None num_classes int

Number of classes. If omitted, inferred as labels.max() + 1.

None device str or device

Device used for metric computation.

"cpu" eps float

Small positive value to avoid division by zero for degenerate class directions.

1e-12

Returns:

Type Description float

Average directional CDNV value over ordered class pairs.

Examples:

import torch
from repx.geometry import compute_directional_cdnv

x = torch.randn(100, 128)
y = torch.randint(0, 10, (100,))

score = compute_directional_cdnv(x, y, num_classes=10)
print(score)

docs.title: compute_etf_deviation
docs.text:
Compute deviation of class means from simplex ETF geometry.

The metric is based on class means. It first centers class means across active classes and L2-normalizes each mean vector. It then compares the resulting Gram matrix to the ETF target Gram matrix:

  • diagonal entries = 1
  • off-diagonal entries = -1 / (C - 1)

where C is the number of active classes (classes that appear in labels). Returned value is the relative Frobenius error:

||G - G_etf||_F / ||G_etf||_F

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default features (Tensor, shape(num_samples, feature_dim))

Embedding matrix.

required labels (Tensor, shape(num_samples))

Class labels for each sample.

required num_classes int

Total number of classes. If omitted, inferred as labels.max() + 1.

None device str or device

Device used for metric computation.

"cpu" eps float

Numerical stability constant used in vector normalization.

1e-12

Returns:

Type Description float

Relative ETF deviation. Lower is better; 0 indicates perfect ETF geometry for active class means.

docs.title: Accuracy
docs.text:
Accuracy-based representation metrics.

OverviewSubmodulesFiles

Transfer-based representation metrics.

This subpackage exposes: - NCCCEvaluator: class-based nearest class center evaluation. - LinearProbeEvaluator: linear-probe transfer evaluation.

No submodules are defined yet.

  • src/repx/transfer/__init__.py

docs.title: LinearProbeEvaluator(device='cpu')
docs.text:
Train and evaluate a linear probe on frozen features.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default device str or device

Device used for training and evaluation.

"cpu"

docs.title: evaluate(train_features, train_labels, test_features, test_labels, num_output_classes, lr=0.0003, epochs=100, n_shots=None, repeat=5, selected_classes=None, optimizer='adam', optimizer_kwargs=None, loss_fn='cross_entropy', loss_fn_kwargs=None)
docs.text:
Train and evaluate a linear probe.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default train_features (Tensor, shape(num_train, feature_dim))

Frozen train embeddings.

required train_labels (Tensor, shape(num_train))

Train labels.

required test_features (Tensor, shape(num_test, feature_dim))

Frozen test embeddings.

required test_labels (Tensor, shape(num_test))

Test labels.

required num_output_classes int

Number of output classes for the linear probe layer.

required lr float

Learning rate for Adam.

3e-4 epochs int

Number of optimization epochs.

100 n_shots int

Number of samples per class for few-shot training. If omitted, full filtered train data is used.

None repeat int

Number of repeated runs.

5 selected_classes sequence[int]

If provided, restrict training/evaluation to this class subset.

None optimizer str or Optimizer class

Optimizer choice used to train the probe.

"adam" optimizer_kwargs mapping

Extra keyword arguments passed to optimizer constructor. If lr is not provided, this method's lr argument is used.

None loss_fn str or nn.Module class

Loss function used for probe training.

"cross_entropy" loss_fn_kwargs mapping

Extra keyword arguments passed to the loss constructor.

None

Returns:

Type Description tuple[float, float]

(mean_train_accuracy, mean_test_accuracy).

docs.title: compute_class_centers(features, labels, n_shots=None, repeat=1, selected_classes=None)
docs.text: Compute class centers from full- or few-shot support data.

docs.title: evaluate(features, labels, n_shots=None, repeat=1, selected_classes=None)
docs.text:
Evaluate accuracy using nearest class centers.

Centers are computed internally by :meth:compute_class_centers from the same input features and labels.
